# Start Somewhere, Iterate

**Flexibility: Expected (SHOULD)**

## The Commitment

It is better to start with an imperfect version and improve it than to wait
for the perfect version that never arrives. Progress through iteration
outperforms progress through perfection.

## What This Means

- Governance objects start at "assess" stage and mature through the lifecycle.
- First versions are explicitly labeled as drafts — not pretending to be final.
- Iteration cycles are short and frequent.
- "Not ready" is never an excuse for "not started."

## Violation Looks Like

A governance framework that has been "in design" for six months with no
operational version. A venture that delays launching because governance
"isn't complete yet." Perfect documentation that nobody uses because it
took too long to write.

## Derived From

- JOS-C11 (Evolutionary Optimization) — iteration IS evolution
- JOS-C09 (Cynefin Framework) — complex problems require probing, not planning
